channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-12669] FLR-EC: recover data from parity code
item.description:
When reading from an EC file, if the read I/O encountered failed OSTs, the IO framework detects the error. The LOV layer will manage the page to read the remaining data pages in the EC chunk and its associated parity from the code component and re-generate the missed data page for the failed OSTs, so that client can read without noticing OSTs’ unavailable. Since the EC parity is computed on a per-block basis (or even strictly one byte or word at a time) across the stripes of the file, the minimum number of pages needed to reconstruct one missing data page is (d-1)+1p, one data page from each remaining stripe plus one parity page (though possibly both parities to allow confirming reconstruction correctness).

For larger reads (~= stripe_size), it will be most efficient to read the full stripe_size of data and parity and reconstruct a whole stripe at a time, rather than doing this on a page-by-page basis. It is likely that the additional data stripes would be needed for the application read() call in any case, so could be considered the same as readahead for the file.
item.summary: FLR-EC: recover data from parity code

status.description: This issue is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

"Zhenyu Xu <bobijam@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63166

Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 706e84a6e73a14f95a6fff33c840618ec5ac1377

"Zhenyu Xu <bobijam@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62791
Subject: LU-12669 ec: make ec_split_stripes() global function
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 73e925a3e686bac05a6e864d88b091803fc9c52a

"Zhenyu Xu <bobijam@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62757
Subject: LU-12669 ec: recover data from parity
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: b677467dd59b0b5cb14b9d5534b4c929c542c3b1

I've been looking at the original patch here comment and the design looks good to me.  The basic approach of mapping things into the paycheck pages seems sound, and I think we can handle direct IO by simply forcing it to fall back to buffered IO when there is a degraded read, since degraded read are not performant anyway.

My only other concern is I want to be sure that we're locking all of the required components together, ie, not lock-read-unlock, lock-read-unlock, but lock-lock, read-read, unlock-unlock so we don't run into any issues with split reads when we eventually support writes.

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51493
Subject: LU-12669 ec: restore unavailable data
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 8226a01cf1e9598312e98cc47bc03d7b6ec7fa76

Bobi Jam (bobijam@hotmail.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/38076
Subject: LU-12669 ec: restore unavailable data

Branch: flr
Current Patch Set: 1
